c1.speaker: narrator
c1.line: The lockup is quiet. Ferris sits deflated on the cot, tail unbrushed — honest, for the first time since the marsh.

c2.speaker: ferris
c2.line: Ferris Mott didn't take your memories, reporter. But two nights before the ceremony, Ferris Mott saw who might have. I was — surveying — by the monument. A figure came across the square. Tall. Rain falling, and him dry under a great posh umbrella.

c3.speaker: ferris
c3.line: And here's the thing that itched me: he didn't pick that lock. He used a key. Smooth as butter. Didn't look around once. Moved like the square belonged to him.

c5.speaker: ferris
c5.line: Because the constable owns a notebook and you own a newspaper, and Ferris Mott knows which one the truth prefers. Also the garden thing. Ferris Mott would like the record to show he intends to fix the garden thing.
